stimuli.description: Blank 4x4 response grid; participant reproduces the previously-shown sequence of black cells via arrow-key navigation + spacebar selection.

response.correct_sequence_js: (() => { try { const rows = jsPsych.data.get().values().filter(r => r.trial_id === 'practice_stim' || r.trial_id === 'test_stim'); const last = rows.slice(-4); if (last.length < 4) return null; return last.map(r => r.spatial_location); } catch (e) { return null; } })()

task_specific.correct_sequence_js: (() => { try { const rows = jsPsych.data.get().values().filter(r => r.trial_id === 'practice_stim' || r.trial_id === 'test_stim'); const last = rows.slice(-4); if (last.length < 4) return null; return last.map(r => r.spatial_location); } catch (e) { return null; } })()

sequence_structure.start_cursor_source: startingCellInGrid (random 0..15, recorded on the response trial); the participant program must compute the arrow-key path from the current cursor to each target and confirm with spacebar
sequence_structure.target_source: spatial_location field of the last 4 presentation rows (practice_stim / test_stim); the vestigial window.currSeq is declared but never populated, so targets are reconstructed from recorded data (mirrors the source's own scoring, which filters *_stim and takes slice(-4))

structure.adaptive_procedure: none (fixed set size 4; simple span is not staircased)

notes.cdp_dwell_ms_derivation: Only one response window exists: responseBlockDuration = 7000 ms. The literal formula min(window)*0.15 = 1050 ms assumes a SINGLE keypress per window; here each response window requires ~10-16 ordered keypresses (arrow navigation + 4 spacebar confirmations) inside the same 7000 ms. Per-keypress budget ~= 7000/16 ~= 440 ms, so 440*0.15 ~= 66 ms; rounded up to 100 ms (>50 ms floor) so total delivery (~1.0-1.6 s) stays well within the 7000 ms window.
notes.platform_export_omitted: runtime.platform_export is intentionally omitted: the response trial (trial_id 'test_trial') records correctness (correct_trial) and per-selection timestamp arrays but NO scalar rt column, so the required declarative rt field cannot be mapped honestly. Analysis for this span task is accuracy-based (correct_trial / partial recall).

phase_detection.complete: (() => { try { if (document.getElementById('finished_message')) return true; const b = document.body ? document.body.innerText : ''; return b.includes('Experiment Complete'); } catch (e) { return false; } })()
phase_detection.feedback: (() => { try { if (document.getElementById('finished_message')) return false; return !!document.querySelector('.picture_box') || (!!document.querySelector('.memory_feedback')) || (!!document.querySelector('.centerbox') && /Press.*enter/i.test(document.querySelector('.centerbox').innerText)); } catch (e) { return false; } })()

timing.trial_context_js: (() => { try { const s = (typeof getExpStage === 'function') ? getExpStage() : ''; const b = (s === 'practice') ? (typeof practiceCount !== 'undefined' ? practiceCount : '') : (typeof testCount !== 'undefined' ? testCount : ''); return 'stage:' + s + ' block:' + b + ' condition:simple'; } catch (e) { return ''; } })()

attention_check.response_js: (() => { try { if (typeof currentAttentionCheckData !== 'undefined' && currentAttentionCheckData && currentAttentionCheckData.A) { return String.fromCharCode(currentAttentionCheckData.A).toLowerCase(); } } catch (e) {} try { const el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-attention-check-rdoc-stimulus'); const t = (el ? el.innerText : '').toLowerCase(); let m = t.match(/press the ([a-z]) key/); if (m) return m[1]; if (t.includes('third to last')) return 'x'; if (t.includes('second to last')) return 'y'; if (t.includes('last letter')) return 'z'; m = t.match(/(first|second|third) letter/); if (m) return { first: 'a', second: 'b', third: 'c' }[m[1]]; } catch (e) {} return null; })()
